name: Pineapple-Carrot Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Sift together flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon; set aside.
2. Combine cooking oil, eggs, and sugar in a mixing bowl. Beat with an electric mixer at medium speed for 2 minutes.
3. Blend in vanilla. Add dry ingredients, carrots, pineapple, coconut, and walnuts to the oil mixture, stirring until well blended.
4. Pour batter into an ungreased 13 x 9 x 2-inch baking pan. Bake in a 350°F oven for 55 minutes, or until a cake tester or wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
5. Place pan on a rack. Immediately pour vanilla glaze over the hot cake.
6. Cool completely.
Vanilla Glaze:
Combine 1 cup sugar, 1 teaspoon baking soda, 1/4 cup butter or regular margarine, 1 tablespoon light corn syrup, 1/2 cup sour milk and 1 teaspoon vanilla in a 2-quart saucepan. Cook over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture comes to a boil. Simmer over low heat for 5 minutes. Remove from heat; immediately pour over hot cake. Note: To make sour milk, place 1 1/2 teaspoons vinegar in a measuring cup. Add enough milk to make 1/2 cup.
